codeReview.summary: The SyncSettingsPage enhancement correctly integrates the F014-BE sync health backend APIs into the UI. It adds queue statistics display, per-module breakdown, a failed items list with retry buttons (admin-only), connection quality indicator, and leverages the pre-existing SyncHistoryPanel with filters. The clear queue button was already present and correctly admin-gated. Code quality is clean, TypeScript types match the backend contract, and validation commands passed.

issues.description: Retry error handling uses bare alert() for failure messages. While functional, this is inconsistent with the app's toast notification pattern used elsewhere in the app (e.g., SyncContext showNotification). Consider switching to a toast or inline banner for better UX.

Sync health data polling is hardcoded to 30 seconds with no cleanup dependency on isOpen/unmount edge-case beyond standard useEffect cleanup. The cleanup is present, which is good, but the interval is not configurable. This is acceptable for current scope.

The electron.d.ts additions include several APIs that are not consumed by this feature (getSyncHealthRetryConfig, getSyncHealthQueueHealth, getSyncHealthDashboardData). They were added as part of the backend contract exposure. This is safe but adds surface area; ideally only used APIs are strictly required in renderer types.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md specifies 'User-facing messages in Indonesian' and the implementation follows this well (e.g., 'Kualitas Koneksi', 'Statistik Queue Sinkronisasi', 'Retry Semua'). This convention is consistently applied in F015-UI.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/renderer/pages/SyncSettingsPage.tsx:284–316 (connection quality labels), 324–327 (queue stats labels), 373–375 (failed items header)

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker SKILL.md instructs workers to use agent-browser for manual verification and document interactive checks. The F015-UI worker transcript indicates that the worker did not perform interactive agent-browser checks (interactiveChecks: []) and instead relied solely on npm run typecheck, npm test, and npm run lint. This is a deviation from the skill's prescribed procedure, though the UI changes are straightforward IPC integrations on an existing page.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff for F015-UI shows interactiveChecks: [] and no agent-browser usage. SKILL.md step 6: 'Manual Verification with agent-browser'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new services or CLI commands were introduced by this feature, so no services.yaml updates were needed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Diff only modifies src/renderer/pages/SyncSettingsPage.tsx and src/renderer/types/electron.d.ts

sharedStateObservations.observation: The existing SyncHistoryPanel component already provided sync history with date/module/status filtering, which the feature description listed as a requirement. The worker correctly identified this and did not duplicate functionality. This shows good codebase awareness.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff: '(5) SyncHistoryPanel already existed with date/module/status filters'.

summary: F015-UI passes review. The SyncSettingsPage enhancements are correctly implemented: queue stats, per-module breakdown, failed items with retry (admin-only), connection quality indicator, and clear queue (already present, admin-only). Sync history filtering is satisfied by the pre-existing SyncHistoryPanel. All validators passed (typecheck, lint, tests). No blocking issues. Minor non-blocking items: alert()-based retry error handling and unused fully typed APIs in electron.d.ts.
